definitions.text: To position or be positioned physically: To support oneself on the feet in an erect position.
examples: Here I stand, wondering what to do next.
I can't reach the celing. Get me a chair to stand on.

definitions.text: To support oneself on the feet in an erect position.
examples: Here I stand, wondering what to do next.
I can't reach the celing. Get me a chair to stand on.

definitions.text: To rise to one’s feet; to stand up.
examples: Stand up, walk to the refrigerator, and get your own snack.
The Commander says we will make our stand here.

definitions.text: A resolute, unwavering position; firm opinion; action for a purpose in the face of opposition.
examples: They took a firm stand against copyright infringement.

definitions.text: A container which stands upright, such as a barrel or cask.
A weight of from two hundred and fifty to three hundred pounds, used in weighing pitch.
